title: ceratophyllum submersum var. granulosum
intro: Ceratophyllum submersum var. granulosum, also known as the hornwort, is a submerged aquatic plant species that belongs to the family Ceratophyllaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Ceratophyllum submersum var. granulosum include its potential to treat wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, making it a valuable herb in folk medicine. Herbal preparations of Ceratophyllum submersum var. granulosum can be made into teas, infusions, or salves to harness its medicinal properties.
